name: Monte Carlo Simulation
applicable_scenarios: estimate expectations / probabilities of a complex stochastic system
python_template:
import numpy as np

def monte_carlo_estimate(f, sampler, n=100000, rng=None):
    rng = rng or np.random.default_rng(0)
    samples = np.asarray([f(sampler(rng)) for _ in range(n)])
    mu = samples.mean()
    se = samples.std(ddof=1) / np.sqrt(n)
    return mu, se
typical_cases: estimating the value of pi via random darts
reliability / failure-rate estimation for engineered systems
common_pitfalls: standard error decreases as 1/sqrt(n) — high accuracy is expensive
poor pseudo-RNG or correlated streams bias results; prefer PCG / Philox
rare events need importance sampling / stratified sampling
always report a Monte Carlo standard error alongside the point estimate
